title: Adam Lilith Confusion
wikitext:
[]: Adam-Lilith Confusion             Quote Post by Dr_Toxicophilous » Mon Sep 10, 2007 5:34 am Okay I'm sure this has been discussed before but I'm new. Why all the confusion about which is which? Which one was found in antarctica and where was the other one found?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 10, 2007 5:56 am Adam was in Antarctica, and Lilith in Hakone. The confusion came because of a scattering of comments by Gendo, and Kaji (either by mistake or on purpose) introducing Lilith as Adam to Misato. Kaworu correctly identifies the angel as Lilith, although not without making the same mistake at first.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Sep 10, 2007 7:44 am My assumption has always been that Gendo wanted to keep everyone out of the loop as to where Adam was. As you know, Adam was central to his plan, and it very well could have been an act of subterfuge. There is also the possibility that he knew of Kaworu's existance, and that Kaji acting as a spy for the government would filter into Seele, sending Kaworu in the wrong direction when attempting to return to Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Sep 10, 2007 1:09 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Kaworu correctly identifies the angel as Lilith, although not without making the same mistake at first.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 10, 2007 8:05 pm Tch, that's entering plothole territory, I shouldn't have mentioned that. Doesn't Kaworu go on saying, "Adam, mother of us all. Should that which was born from Adam return to Adam, even though it would destroy mankind? .... No/This is different! This is...Lilith? I understand now, Lilim!" *Yui-sama breaks in on cue* Plothole or not, Kaworu did seem to think that Lilith was Adam for just that much time, but he realized his mistake in a matter of...ten seconds?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 11, 2007 12:26 am I'd be repeating myself, so just see this thread: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=2095

[]:             Quote Post by Dr_Toxicophilous » Tue Sep 11, 2007 1:00 am Seems to me like Anno changed it at the last minute. In the Manga they're actually still refering to it as Adam aren't they? When did they say Lilith was found in Hakone?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Sep 11, 2007 6:12 am Dr_Toxicophilous wrote: Seems to me like Anno changed it at the last minute. In the Manga they're actually still refering to it as Adam aren't they? When did they say Lilith was found in Hakone?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 16, 2007 11:21 pm well if they DID trick Kowaro into thinking that Lilith was actually Adam, then why did all the other Angels go for Central Dogma and not Gendos hand and wherever Adam was before Kaji gave it do Gendo? which leades to the question, which do the Angels need to cause 3I? Adam or Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 17, 2007 6:03 am Ka-wo-ru , not Kowaro! According to the CI, some Angels were pretty much doing what they liked while others were aiming specifically for Lilith. To have Kaworu get the Angel wrong until the very last moment is very unusual, since he's got Adam's soul inside him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:21 am sorry, typing too fast. Gaghiel was smart enough to go for Adam

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:30 am then to add to the lilith adam confusion there was that mis translated line by mrs.lee in the american version that confused many. See: [url]http://www.evaotaku.com/misatolines.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:38 am Really? It came as second nature to me that Gendo was being a dirty liar about Adam and Lilith, the first time I saw Lilith on the cross. He put Adam in his hand, for chrissakes. Even Gaghiel, like Sachiel_13 pointed out, was smart enough to go for the real one. I think Gendo and Kaji knew damn well which was which.

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Mon Sep 17, 2007 9:37 am Trigger's Elysium wrote: I think Gendo and Kaji knew damn well which was which.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Sep 17, 2007 9:45 am Sandoz wrote: So Kaji lied to Misato, then?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:21 pm well if they DID trick Kowaro into thinking that Lilith was actually Adam, then why did all the other Angels go for Central Dogma and not Gendos hand and wherever Adam was before Kaji gave it do Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 17, 2007 7:52 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Sandoz wrote: So Kaji lied to Misato, then?

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Sep 17, 2007 7:58 pm gg retcons

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:23 pm Defectron wrote: well if they DID trick Kowaro into thinking that Lilith was actually Adam, then why did all the other Angels go for Central Dogma and not Gendos hand and wherever Adam was before Kaji gave it do Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Sep 17, 2007 9:05 pm To answer the above two things: FGP wrote: Series creator Hideaki Anno was extensively interviewed by the video game developers, so the information contained within the "Classified Information" files should probably be considered "second-tier" canon: it is fairly accurate and reveals many things which the series only vaguely explains, however it also mentions a few things which contradict things depicted in the series. For example, the series states that all Angels are trying to return to Adam, but the Classified Information files say that some Angels were trying to reach Lilith, while others were trying to reach Adam, and some simply had no purpose at all. Thus the information contained inside should probably be taken with a grain of salt, but individual facts revealed within the files are probably trustworthy so long as they do not directly conflict with information from the series itself.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 10, 2007 5:56 am Adam was in Antarctica, and Lilith in Hakon
